source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Above when He said, Sacrifice and offering, and burnt-offerings, and [offering] for sin, Thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure [therein], which are offered by the Law, then said He, Lo!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I come to do Thy will, O God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He taketh away the first, that He may establish the second.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By the which will we are sanctified, by the offering of the body of Jesus Christ, once for all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And every Priest standeth daily ministering, and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But this [man] after He had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God, from henceforth expecting till His enemies be made His footstool.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In what has gone before he had shown that the sacrifices were unavailing for perfect purification, and were a type, and greatly defective.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since then there was this objection to his argument, If they are types, how is it that, after the truth is come, they have not ceased, nor given place, but are still performed? he here accordingly labors at this very point, showing that they are no longer performed, even as a figure, for God does not accept them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this again he shows not from the New [Testament], but from the prophets, bringing forward from times of old the strongest testimony, that it [the old system] comes to an end, and ceases, and that they do all in vain, “alway resisting the Holy Ghost.” ( Acts vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he shows over and above that they cease not now [only], but at the very coming of the Messiah, nay rather, even before His coming: and how it was that Christ did not abolish them at the last, but they were abolished first, and then He came; first they were made to cease, and then He appeared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That they might not say, Even without this sacrifice, and by means of those, we could have been well pleasing unto God, He waited for these sacrifices to be convicted [of weakness], and then He appeared; for (He says) “sacrifice and offering Thou wouldest not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hereby He took all away; and having spoken generally, He says also particularly, “In burnt-offerings and [sacrifice] for sin Thou hadst no pleasure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But “the offering” was everything except the sacrifice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Then said I, Lo!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of whom was this spoken? of none other than the Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he does not blame those who offer, showing that it is not because of their wickednesses that He does not accept them, as He says elsewhere, but because the thing itself has been convicted for the future and shown to have no strength, nor any suitableness to the times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then has this to do with the “sacrifices” being offered “oftentimes”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not only from their being “oftentimes” [offered] (he means) is it manifest that they are weak, and that they effected nothing; but also from God’s not accepting them, as being unprofitable and useless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And in another place it is said, “If Thou hadst desired sacrifice I would have given it.” ( Ps. li.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore by this also he makes it plain that He does not desire it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore sacrifices are not God’s will, but the abolition of sacrifices.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore they sacrifice contrary to His will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “To do Thy will”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: To give up Myself, He means: This is the will of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By which Will we are sanctified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Or he even means something still further, that the sacrifices do not make men clean, but the Will of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore to offer sacrifice is not the will of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And why dost thou wonder that it is not the will of God now, when it was not His will even from the beginning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “who,” saith He, “hath required this at your hands?” ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How then did He Himself enjoin it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as Paul says, “I would that all men were even as I myself” ( 1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 7 ), in respect of continence, and again says, “I will that the younger women marry, bear children” ( 1 Tim. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ); and lays down two wills, yet the two are not his own, although he commands; but the one indeed is his own, and therefore he lays it down without reasons; while the other is not his own, though he wishes it, and therefore it is added with a reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For having previously accused them, because “they had waxed wanton against Christ” ( 1 Tim. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 11 ), he then says, “I will that the younger women marry, bear children.” ( 1 Tim. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So in this place also it was not His leading will that the sacrifices should be offered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, as He says, “I wish not the death of the sinner, as that he should turn unto [Me] and live” ( Ezek. xxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 11 ): and in another place He says that He not only wished, but even desired this: and yet these are contrary to each other: for intense wishing is desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How then dost Thou “not wish”? how dost Thou in another place “desire,” which is a sign of vehement wishing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So is it in this case also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By the which will we are sanctified,” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How sanctified? “by the offering of the Body of Jesus Christ once for all.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (To stand therefore is a sign of ministering; accordingly to sit, is a sign of being ministered unto.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But this [man] after He had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God, from henceforth expecting till His enemies be made His footstool.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14, 15 ) “For by one offering He hath perfected forever them that are sanctified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whereof the Holy Ghost also is a witness to us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He had said that those [sacrifices] are not offered; he reasoned from what is written, [and] from what is not written; moreover also he put forward the prophetic word which says, “sacrifice and offering Thou wouldest not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He had said that He had forgiven their sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Again this also He proves from the testimony of what is written, for “the Holy Ghost” (he says) “is a witness to us: for after that He had said,” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 16–18 ) “This is the covenant, that I will make with them, after those days, saith the Lord: I will put My laws into their hearts, and in their minds will I write them, and their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Now where remission of these is there is no more offering for sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So then He forgave their sins, when He gave the Covenant, and He gave the Covenant by sacrifice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If therefore He forgave the sins through the one sacrifice, there is no longer need of a second.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “He sat down on the right hand of God, from henceforth expecting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why the delay? “that His enemies be put under His feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For by one offering He hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But perhaps some one might say; Wherefore did He not put them under at once?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the sake of the faithful who should afterwards be brought forth and born.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whence then [does it appear] that they shall be put under?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By the saying “He sat down.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He called to mind again that testimony which saith, “until I put the enemies under His feet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (See above, i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But His enemies are the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then since he had said, “Till His enemies be put under His feet,” and they [these enemies] were vehemently urgent, therefore he introduces all his discourse concerning faith after this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But who are the enemies?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All unbelievers: the dæmons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And intimating the greatness of their subjection, he said not “are subjected,” but “are put under His feet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not therefore be of [the number of] His enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For not they alone are enemies, the unbelievers and Jews, but those also who are full of unclean living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, for neither can it be.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then (you say)? this is not a ground of blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nay rather, it is very much a ground of blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the wicked man as long as he is wicked, cannot be subject [to God’s law]; he can however change and become good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then cast out carnal minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what are carnal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whatever makes the body flourish and do well, but injures the soul: as for instance, wealth, luxury, glory (all these things are of the flesh), carnal love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not then love gain, but ever follow after poverty: for this is a great good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But (you say) it makes one humble and of little account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: [True:] for we have need of this, for it benefits us much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Poverty” (it is said) “humbles a man.” ( Prov. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again Christ [says], “Blessed are the poor in spirit.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou then grieve because thou art upon a path leading to virtue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou not know that this gives us great confidence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But, one says, “the wisdom of the poor man is despised.” ( Eccles. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again another says, “Give me neither riches nor poverty” ( Prov. xxx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 8 ), and, “Deliver me from the furnace of poverty.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (See Isa. xlviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, if riches and poverty are from the Lord, how can either poverty or riches be an evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then were these things said?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: They were said under the Old [Covenant], where there was much account made of wealth, where there was great contempt of poverty, where the one was a curse and the other a blessing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But now it is no longer so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But wilt thou hear the praises of poverty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Christ sought after it, and saith, “But the Son of Man hath not where to lay His head.” ( Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again He said to His disciples, “Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor two coats.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And Paul in writing said, “As having nothing and yet possessing all things.” ( 2 Cor. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And Peter said to him who was lame from his birth, “Silver and gold have I none.” ( Acts iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea and under the Old [Covenant] itself, where wealth was held in admiration, who were the admired?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Was not Elijah, who had nothing save the sheepskin?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Was not Elisha?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Was not John?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let no man then be humiliated on account of his poverty: It is not poverty which humiliates, but wealth, which compels us to have need of many, and forces us to be under obligations to many?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what could be poorer than Jacob (tell me), who said, “If the Lord give me bread to eat, and raiment to put on”? ( Gen. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Were Elijah and John then wanting in boldness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Did not the one reprove Ahab, and the other Herod?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The latter said, “It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother Philip’s wife.” ( Mark vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And Elias said to Ahab with boldness, “It is not I that trouble Israel, but thou and thy father’s house.” ( 1 Kings xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that this especially produces boldness; poverty [I mean]?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For while the rich man is a slave, being subject to loss, and in the power of every one wishing to do him hurt, he who has nothing, fears not confiscation, nor fine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So, if poverty had made men wanting in boldness Christ would not have sent His disciples with poverty to a work requiring great boldness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the poor man is very strong, and has nothing wherefrom he may be wronged or evil entreated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But the rich man is assailable on every side: just in the same way as one would easily catch a man who was dragging many long ropes after him, whereas one could not readily lay hold on a naked man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So here also it falls out in the case of the rich man: slaves, gold, lands, affairs innumerable, innumerable cares, difficult circumstances, necessities, make him an easy prey to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let no man then henceforth esteem poverty a cause of disgrace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if virtue be there, all the wealth of the world is neither clay, nor even a mote in comparison of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This then let us follow after, if we would enter into the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, He saith, “Sell that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven.” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “It is hard for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou see that even if we have it not, we ought to draw it to us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So great a good is Poverty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it guides us by the hand, as it were, on the path which leads to Heaven, it is an anointing for the combat, an exercise great and admirable, a tranquil haven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But (you say) I have need of many [things], and am unwilling to receive a favor from any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nevertheless, even in this respect the rich man is inferior to thee; for thou perhaps askest the favor for thy support, but he shamelessly [asks] for ten thousand things for covetousness’ sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that it is the rich that are in need of many [persons], yea oftentimes those who are unworthy of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For instance, they often stand in need of those who are in the rank of soldiers, or of slaves: but the poor man has no need even of the Emperor himself, and if he should need him, he is admired because he has brought himself down to this, when he might have been rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let no man then accuse poverty as being the cause of innumerable evils, nor let him contradict Christ, who declared it to be the perfection of virtue, saying, “If thou wilt be perfect.” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this He both uttered in His words, and showed by His acts, and taught by His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us therefore follow after poverty, it is the greatest good to the sober-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Perhaps some of those who hear me, avoid it as a thing of ill omen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I do not doubt it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this disease is great among most men, and such is the tyranny of wealth, that they cannot even as far as words endure the renunciation of it, but avoid it as of ill omen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Far be this from the Christian’s soul: for nothing is richer than he who chooses poverty of his own accord, and with a ready mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I will tell you, and if you please, I will prove that he who chooses poverty of his own accord is richer even than the king himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he indeed needs many [things], and is in anxiety, and fears lest the supplies for the army should fail him; but the other has enough of everything, and fears about nothing, and if he fears, it is not about so great matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Who then, tell me, is the rich man? he who is daily asking, and earnestly laboring to gather much together, and fears lest at any time he should fall short, or he who gathers nothing together, and is in great abundance and hath need of no one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is virtue and the fear of God, and not possessions which give confidence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For these even enslave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is said, “Gifts and presents blind the eyes of the wise, and like a muzzle on the mouth turn away reproofs.” ( Ecclus. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Consider how the poor man Peter chastised the rich Ananias.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Was not the one rich and the other poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But behold the one speaking with authority and saying, “Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much” ( Acts v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 8 ), and the other saying with submission, “Yea, for so much.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And who (you say) will grant to me to be as Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is open to thee to be as Peter if thou wilt; cast away what thou hast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Disperse, give to the poor” ( Ps. cxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 9 ), follow Christ, and thou shalt be such as he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How? he (you say) wrought miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is it this then, tell me, which made Peter an object of admiration, or the boldness which arose from his manner of life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou not hear Christ saying, “Rejoice not because the devils are subject unto you; If thou wilt be perfect [&c].” ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hear what Peter says: “Silver and gold have I none, but what I have I give thee.” ( Acts iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If any man have silver and gold, he hath not those other gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why is it then, you say, that many have neither the one nor the other?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because they are not voluntarily poor: since they who are voluntarily poor have all good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For although they do not raise up the dead nor the lame, yet, what is greater than all; they have confidence towards God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: They will hear in that day that blessed voice, “Come, ye blessed of My Father,” (what can be better than this?) “inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world: for I was an hungered and ye gave Me meat: I was thirsty and ye gave Me drink: I was a stranger and ye took Me in: I was naked and ye clothed Me: I was sick and in prison and ye visited Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then flee from covetousness, that we may attain to the kingdom [of Heaven].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us feed the poor, that we may feed Christ: that we may become fellow-heirs with Him in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost, be glory, power, honor, now and for ever and world without end.
